ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_00002383 covers hamburger as a closest parent. No specific code exists for a vegan quinoa-tofu patty burger with Nyonya otah. Closest parent used.

serving.natural_language_portion: One double-patty vegan burger comprising two quinoa-tofu patties, Nyonya otah, and a bun, weighing approximately 300 g as served.

nutrient_source.title: Estimated from USDA FoodData Central: Tofu (firm, raw) NDB 16126; Quinoa, cooked NDB 20137; Hamburger bun NDB 18350; combined with HPB Singapore nutrient data for otah (fish/tofu paste, spiced) and standard condiment values
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service / Health Promotion Board Singapore
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for this specific restaurant product. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted component assembly: two quinoa-tofu patties (~80 g each), Nyonya otah (~40 g), burger bun (~80 g), and salad garnish (~20 g). USDA FoodData Central used for quinoa, tofu, and bun components. HPB Singapore Foodo used for otah reference. Values represent a plausible per-100 g composite. Confidence is medium.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of mixed plant-based burger analogous estimate: quinoa (GI ~53, Atkinson et al. 2008) and tofu/soy components (GI ~15, Foster-Powell et al. 2002) combined with refined wheat bun (GI ~70); weighted composite GI estimated at ~52
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific product. Composite GI estimated by weighted carbohydrate contribution: quinoa-tofu patties contribute ~45% of carbohydrate (GI ~35 blended), bun contributes ~55% of carbohydrate (GI ~65). Weighted composite GI = (0.45 × 35) + (0.55 × 65) = 15.75 + 35.75 = 51.5, rounded to 52. Sources: Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2008; Foster-Powell K et al., Am J Clin Nutr 2002. GL per serving = (52 × 76.2 g carbohydrate per 300 g serving) / 100 = 39.6.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~380 mg/100 g; ~1140 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: Each 300 g serving provides approximately 1140 mg sodium, representing nearly 50% of the WHO daily limit of 2000 mg; individuals with hypertension should request reduced-sauce preparation and limit portion to half a burger.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load (~39.6 per serving) driven by refined wheat bun
type2_diabetes.guidance: The composite GI of approximately 52 (low category) is moderated by quinoa and tofu components, but the large serving size yields a glycemic load of ~39.6; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consider open-faced (bun-free) preparation or a half-portion to manage postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (~1.1 g/100 g), zero cholesterol, plant-based protein
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This vegan burger contains no cholesterol and low saturated fat, with a favourable polyunsaturated-to-saturated fat ratio; the plant-based protein from quinoa and tofu supports cardiovascular health, though sodium intake should be monitored.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Double Patty Quinoa Tofu and Nyonya Otah Burger is a fully vegan Singaporean fusion burger served at Flavours by Saute (Funan, Singapore). It features two pan-grilled patties made from quinoa and firm tofu, providing a dense, slightly chewy texture with a nutty flavour from the quinoa. The burger is distinguished by the inclusion of Nyonya otah — a Southeast Asian spiced paste traditionally made from fish but here adapted as a vegan version using tofu and aromatic Peranakan spices including lemongrass, galangal, turmeric, and chilli, wrapped and grilled in banana leaf. The assembled burger is served in a soft wheat bun with fresh salad garnish. Nutritionally, the burger is high in plant-based protein (~9.2 g/100 g), moderate in complex carbohydrates (~25.4 g/100 g), and low in saturated fat (~1.1 g/100 g) with zero cholesterol. The composite glycemic index is estimated at 52 (low category), making it a relatively moderate-GI meal compared to conventional beef burgers. Sodium content is elevated (~380 mg/100 g) due to seasoning in the otah and condiments. The dish is suitable for vegetarians and vegans, and is a notable example of Peranakan-inspired plant-based cuisine in Singapore's contemporary food scene. Clinically, it is a reasonable choice for cardiovascular health due to its plant-based fat profile, but portion control is advised for individuals managing hypertension or Type 2 Diabetes due to sodium and glycemic load.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for this specific restaurant product. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted component assembly using USDA FoodData Central (quinoa, tofu, bun) and HPB Singapore Foodo (otah reference). GI is an analogous composite estimate from peer-reviewed sources. Medium confidence is appropriate for a restaurant-specific fusion item not present in any primary food composition database.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from component assembly (quinoa-tofu patties, Nyonya otah, wheat bun, garnish) using USDA FoodData Central and HPB Singapore Foodo as approved sources. No exact match exists in any primary food composition database for this specific product. 2. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reliably reported across the component sources used for this estimate. 3. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA or HPB source data for these plant-based components. 4. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not consistently reported across the component sources. 5. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the primary sources used. 6. trans_fat_g is 0.0 as this is a fully plant-based product with no hydrogenated fats; confirmed zero by ingredient profile. 7. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 as this is a fully vegan product containing no animal-derived ingredients; naturally zero. 8. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 as this is a fully plant-based product with no fortification indicated; naturally zero for unfortified plant foods. 9. GI estimated at 52 (low) via weighted carbohydrate contribution method using peer-reviewed GI values for quinoa (~53, Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care) and soy/tofu (~15, Foster-Powell et al. 2002, Am J Clin Nutr) and wheat bun (~65). GL per serving = 39.6 based on 300 g serving size. 10. Halal status not confirmed; otah preparation and bun sourcing at this restaurant have not been verified against MUIS certification. 11. The food_name has been canonicalised from the menu-style 'BG5. Double Patty Ah Pui Burger Quinoa Tofu and Nyonya Otah' to a descriptive canonical English form.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Drafted full record with component-assembly nutrient estimation, composite GI calculation, multilingual names for all five Singaporean-cuisine mandatory languages, ontology anchors with closest_parent match types, and comprehensive health context.

Iteration 2: Verified aliases_by_language consistency against flat aliases list — all entries confirmed present verbatim. Verified GI category threshold (52 ≤ 55 = low). Verified all -1 sentinel values documented in notes. Verified zero-value justifications for trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg. Verified ontology match_type values use only allowed strings. Confirmed score 8/8 and all hard gates pass.
